events.title: üåà SIX The Musical
events.location: Steven Tanger Center for the Performing Arts
events.description:
It‚Äôs time to take back the microphone and reclaim your crown!

We are SO excited to give away 4 tickets to the global sensation SIX for the opening night performance on Tuesday, May 5th, at the Tanger Center!

From Tudor Queens to Pop Icons, the SIX wives of Henry VIII remix five hundred years of historical heartbreak into a euphoric celebration of 21st-century power. The New York Times says SIX ‚ÄúTOTALLY RULES!‚Äù and The Washington Post hails it as ‚Äúexactly the kind of energizing, inspirational illumination this town aches for!"

To enter: Tell us about a moment you felt fierce, stood up for yourself, or took back your narrative. We want to hear those "reclaim your crown" moments!

Two winners will be chosen to snag 2 tickets each for the May 5th show.

Show Details: Steven Tanger Center for the Performing Arts Tuesday, May 5th (Show runs May 5-10).

 If you can't make the opening night, you can get tickets from the link in the comments.

We can‚Äôt wait to read your stories below. Let‚Äôs show them how Greensboro shines!

#SIXTheMusical #GreensboroPride #IMPAQTGSO #TangerCenter #ReclaimYourCrown

events.title: üåà Lesbian Visibility Day Celebration
events.description:
üß°ü§çüíñ Today we celebrate Lesbian Visibility Day!!!üéâüéâüéâ

A moment to honor the strength, love, resilience, and contributions of lesbian women everywhere. Visibility isn‚Äôt just about being seen, it‚Äôs about being recognized, valued, and uplifted in every space we enter.

As trailblazer Sally Ride once said:
‚ÄúYou can‚Äôt be what you can‚Äôt see.‚Äù

That truth still resonates. Visibility creates possibility. It opens doors for the next generation to live boldly, authentically, and without apology.

Today, we celebrate:
üß° The stories that have been told, and the ones still waiting to be heard
ü§ç The courage it takes to live openly and honestly
üíñ The community that continues to build spaces of love, safety, and empowerment

To every lesbian who has ever questioned if they belong, you do. Your existence matters. Your love matters. Your visibility matters.

Let‚Äôs continue to show up, speak out, and stand proud, not just today, but every day.

#LesbianVisibilityDay #VisibilityMatters #Pride365 #GSOPride

events.title: üé® Public Open Studio Hours
events.description:
MEET MACY ‚ú® Current Artist in Residence, as part of the GPS Student Residency Program | Summer Studios: Arts on Site! ‚ú®

About Macy macyjart 
Macy James (BFA Sculpture & Ceramics) will explore various materials that she has yet to try, though has long wanted to. Within this residency, Macy plans to work with more accessible material specifically, in addition to those she is accustomed to, like found and second-hand goods sourced from local places - materials that do not require large shops or expensive tools. Another material interest is found wood, like driftwood, to experiment with wood carving or wood burning alongside bead and wire sculptures, which she presently has no experience working with. The hope is that this will help her develop a healthy habit of creating art with widely accessible materials and make her an overall more multifaceted artist.

About the Student Residency Program:
Designed to support UNCG undergraduate student artists (BA + BFA) striving to develop, adapt, and/or reinvent their creative process and to promote artistic growth and development, artists are afforded two-weeks of uninterrupted research and development, coupled with financial support and public presentational platforms to share their creative work. 

Join our AIRs in their studio practices üìÖ:
May 11-22, 2026

Public Open Studio Hours:
Monday, May 18th | 12-3P
Wednesday, May 20th | 2-5P

Closing Reception + Open Studio: 
Friday, May 22nd | 6-8P

Learn more by clicking ‚ÄúHappening Now‚Äù in the Linktree above in our profile

events.title: üé® GPS Student Residency Program: Summer Studios
events.description:
MEET ELISSA ‚ú® Current Artist in Residence, as part of the GPS Student Residency Program | Summer Studios: Arts on Site! ‚ú®

About Elissa elissa.artist 
Elissa Ukhueligbe (BFA Painting) plans to expand their practice through video and sound. The proposed idea is to animate photos into video and projections, mimicking a low ‚Äúframes per second.‚Äù Using film, photos, drawings, or smaller paintings, they would mix video/sound with traditional art. Elissa is a multi-media artist that uses portraits of women to communicate complex ideas about femininity, self-expression, and gendered norms. They are interested in psychological exploration; how the media we consume affects us internally and externally.

About the Student Residency Program:
Designed to support UNCG undergraduate student artists (BA + BFA) striving to develop, adapt, and/or reinvent their creative process and to promote artistic growth and development, artists are afforded two-weeks of uninterrupted research and development, coupled with financial support and public presentational platforms to share their creative work.

events.title: üé® Homebody
events.description:
Thank you to everyone who joined us for the reception of Homebody by Kathryn Hood!ü•Ç

The exhibition is still open until 5pm ‚Äî don‚Äôt miss out on experiencing Homebody

************************
NOW OPEN @ GPS
Homebody

MFA Thesis Exhibition
by Kathryn Hood

Exhibition: April 28 - May 2, 2026
Reception: Friday, May 1st, 6-8p

Gallery Open Hours:
Tuesday - Friday 12-5p
Saturday, 2-5p

Homebody is an exhibition of collages and sculptures in which Kathryn Hood carves her perceptions of tense muscles into paper and upholstery foam. She embraces the body-mind connection, an essential tool for self-understanding, as she houses ungrounded avatars within handmade paper and muscular furnishings outside of formal traditions. Hood composes the interior feelings of ideas & emotions with the exterior feeling of tension. This duality of perception reflects the disparate nature of themes explored, such as femininity, domesticity, and the performance of identity.

Homebody is the residence of textured perceptions. This space is an act of resensitization.

Learn more by clicking ‚ÄúHappening Now‚Äù in the Linktree above in our profile

events.title: üé® Garden of Corporeal Fantasy
events.description:
Thank you to everyone who joined us in celebrating the reception Garden of Corporeal Fantasy by Victoria Mercado-Lues

The exhibition is still open until 5pm ‚Äî step inside before it closes

************************
NOW OPEN @ GPS
Garden of Corporeal Fantasy
MFA Thesis Exhibition
by Victoria Mercado-Lues

Exhibition: April 21 - 25, 2026
Reception: Friday, April 24th, 6-8p

Gallery Open Hours:
Tuesday - Friday 12-5p
Saturday, 2-5p

Brightly saturated and fantastical, Garden of Corporeal Fantasy explores the meeting of nature and flesh in oil paintings and ceramics. With invented creatures and visceral biological imagery, the works reframe the gaze towards the bodily through a lens of wonder ‚Äî a less punitive lens than the centuries-long and still recurring traditions of bodies as commodified, objectified, or sinful.

This exhibition addresses our discomfort surrounding the body by combining it with the godly imposing presence of the landscape, like waterfalls and billowing clouds. In this combination, the work is imbued with a devotional, almost sacred quality, borrowing visuals of the holy and filling it with the biological. The sticky, hairy, fleshy becomes something worth staring at with open fascination, the same way we are in awe of exotic plants, or deep-sea creatures, illuminating the sublime inherent in the corporeal.
Learn more by clicking ‚ÄúHappening Now‚Äù in the Linktree above in our profile

events.title: üé® Death Circle
events.description:
ùîáùî¢ùîûùî±ùî• ‚Ñ≠ùî¶ùîØùîÝùî©ùî¢, ùî±ùî¨ùî°ùîûùî∂ ùî£ùîØùî¨ùî™ 5 ùî±ùî¨ 7 ùîìùîê 

So many of us are carrying a lot right now. Personal losses, collective grief, the ache of what is happening in the world, and the grief we feel for our more-than-human kin and planet. We were never meant to carry any of this alone. What does it mean to live and die consciously? This circle is an offering to unburden ourselves in shared community and, in doing so, perhaps find more spaciousness and imaginative possibilities. 

Together we will co-create a sacred space to remember that these tender conversations about death, grief, cycles of change, and all of our connections in this web of existence once lived at the center of our communities. They are an integral part of our collective wellbeing. 

Death Circle is not a therapeutic setting or a grief support group, but an invitation to be present with one another as we explore these topics and reconnect more deeply with ourselves, each other, and the world around us. We welcome your curiosities, stories, questions, as well as your silence. There is no pressure to speak. All of it belongs üñ§

Our hope is that this offering fosters clarity, nurtures care, and deepens our relational intimacy. 

Death Circle will be held on the last Sunday of every month, from 5-7pm. We‚Äôll have coffee and donuts, but feel free to bring your own snacks or any comfort items that support you.

We look forward to seeing you there ‚Äî 
Joshua & Bri

events.title: üé® Advance Base & Moontype with likewindowpanes
events.description:
advance_base & moontypo are coming back to Greensboro with local support from likewindowpanes on Wednesday, February 25th üëæüé∂üéπ

Doors at 7pm and music at 8pm // $12 suggested 

This is going to be a really sweet show. We‚Äôre super thrilled to have these folks back in Greensboro. If you missed Advance Base for his winter tour in 2024 or Moontype last summer, this is your chance to see them both with Greensboro‚Äôs own, Panes. 

Advance Base is the melancholic soft rock recording project of Chicago, IL singer/songwriter Owen Ashworth (formerly of Casiotone for the Painfully Alone). Using a two-handed arsenal of electric piano, Omnichord, samplers, effect pedals & drum machines, Ashworth builds minimalist, heavy-hearted, nostalgia-obsessed ballads around his conversational baritone. The warm, electronic sound of Advance Base has been described as ‚Äúlo-fi,‚Äù ‚Äúdepressed‚Äù & ‚Äúweirdly uplifting.‚Äù Advance Base released Horrible Occurrences in 2024.

Moontype is songwriter Margaret McCarthy‚Äôs dreamworld come to life. Based in Chicago, Illinois, the band features Emerson Hunton on drums, and Joe Suihkonen and Andrew Clinkman on guitar. Quiet emotions that McCarthy explores with her bass become, with the band, as large and as musically complex as a mood itself: cathartic, angsty, and unmet. Moontype released their debut album, Bodies of Water, in 2021 to critical acclaim.

panes is minimal introspective music with an engaging, dreamy listlessness. ever careening toward some kind of undiscovered self epiphany.

Flyer by: likewindowpanes

events.title: üé® DAGGERTEETH
events.location: Irving Park Art & Frame, 2800 Lawndale Dr. Ste 107, Greensboro, NC
events.description:
DAGGERTEETH TOMORROW TOMORROW TOMORROW.
.
ARE Y'ALL PUMPED CUZ WE CAN'T WAIT TO OPEN OUR DOORS TO YOU TOMORROW!
.
Teeth don't lie. They bite, they tear, they mark what they touch.

Traditional printmaking techniques meet zine culture's raw urgency. Punk aesthetics slash through the white cube aesthetic and screams to be heard.

Every piece here carries evidence of hands‚Äîink rolled, paper pressed, screens burned, images carved. The tactility of making things physical in a digital age. These works don't apologize for their rough edges. They weaponize them.

DAGGERTEETH celebrates artists who create outside institutional approval, distribution systems built on photocopiers and kitchen tables, aesthetics born from necessity and defiance. The underground made visible.

Sharp work for sharp teeth.

**February 21, 2026 | 5PM-8PM**

Irving Park Art & Frame

2800 Lawndale Dr. Ste 107, Greensboro, NC

events.title: üéµ Live Music Night with Tyler Millard, Tom Troyer, Allyn Raney, Drake Duffer
events.description:
Tomorrow, Tuesday the 9th!
Doors at 6pm
Show at 7pm

Tyler Millard is half of the husband and wife duo The Ghosts of Liberty. He discovered his love for creating music almost by accident, only picking up the guitar in 2006 as a hobby to pursue as he slowly lost his vision to a degenerative retinal disease. But what started as a hobby eventually became an obsession. Today, Tyler spends most of his days hacking his way through technology that was never truly designed for the blind in an effort to create music that is simultaneously substantive and catchy.

Tom Troyer is the ‚ÄúArtist in Resonance‚Äù at Back Table Where he hosts the Monday Night Open Mic, curates the Silo Series, a genre blending and community building monthly First Friday gathering. And he coordinates the weekly sound bath ambient music for Ground and Groove yoga Wednesdays. His next single ‚ÄúLet Yourself Move On‚Äù comes out April 30.

Allyn Raney is a performing musician and singer/songwriter from Winston-Salem, North Carolina. In a genre best described as soulful Americana, Allyn blends a variety of influences into a style that is completely her own. At 20 years old, she has performed on hundreds of stages, and is passionate about sharing the joy that music brings her personally with those who are open to listening.

Drake Duffer is a musician and songwriter from Winston-Salem, NC. A classical guitarist by trade, he grew up playing folk and bluegrass in the NC Piedmont, and enjoys weaving the two genres together. He is a founding member of the bands Early Gray and Mount Rosco.

Mason P. Winfree is an award-winning old-time musician and flatfoot dancer who has earned numerous ribbons, including first-place honors, at bluegrass and old-time fiddlers‚Äô conventions across North Carolina and Southwest Virginia, such as the Galax Old Fiddlers Convention and the Mount Airy Fiddlers Convention. Over the years, he has performed with several groups and has shared his craft by teaching and showcasing flatfoot dancing both nationally and abroad, including on PBS‚Äôs Song of the Mountains. *

Folksinger Jodi Jones is determined to boldly dream a better tomorrow in the wake of a broken and weary world. Deeply rooted in the musical traditions of her childhood home in the hills of Western Pennsylvania, her work is a poetic and unflinching account of the beauty and hardship of working class life in America. With defiant wit and innate musicality, she beautifies the bitter truths of our modern age into song as an act of love and solidarity. Art may not save us from our problems, but Jones believes it is crucial to surviving them with our humanity intact.
To sing is to live, to live is to hope, and to hope is to find the strength to fight another day in these dark and uncertain times. With guitar, banjo, fiddle, and even dulcimer in hand, she seeks to alchemize pain into purpose and break old ground to plant new seeds of hope and change wherever she goes.
Jack Romano & The Romanaires create a mythic sound of the 20th century as a backdrop for stories from modern American life.

Jack was born in the New Jersey highlands, a unique cultural¬Ýregion whose ‚Äúhill people‚Äù share as much in common with Appalachian communities in West Virginia as with cities like New York City and Philadelphia. He grew up listening to artists like Johnny Cash, John Denver, and Gene Autry, alongside Frank Sinatra,¬ÝDion & the Belmonts, and The Spinners.

events.title: üéµ Concert: Tyler Millard, Tom Troyer, Allyn Raney, Drake Duffer
events.description:
This coming Tuesday the 9th!
Doors at 6pm
Show at 7pm

Tyler Millard is half of the husband and wife duo The Ghosts of Liberty. He discovered his love for creating music almost by accident, only picking up the guitar in 2006 as a hobby to pursue as he slowly lost his vision to a degenerative retinal disease. But what started as a hobby eventually became an obsession. Today, Tyler spends most of his days hacking his way through technology that was never truly designed for the blind in an effort to create music that is simultaneously substantive and catchy.

Tom Troyer is the ‚ÄúArtist in Resonance‚Äù at Back Table Where he hosts the Monday Night Open Mic, curates the Silo Series, a genre blending and community building monthly First Friday gathering. And he coordinates the weekly sound bath ambient music for Ground and Groove yoga Wednesdays. His next single ‚ÄúLet Yourself Move On‚Äù comes out April 30.

Allyn Raney is a performing musician and singer/songwriter from Winston-Salem, North Carolina. In a genre best described as soulful Americana, Allyn blends a variety of influences into a style that is completely her own. At 20 years old, she has performed on hundreds of stages, and is passionate about sharing the joy that music brings her personally with those who are open to listening.

Drake Duffer is a musician and songwriter from Winston-Salem, NC. A classical guitarist by trade, he grew up playing folk and bluegrass in the NC Piedmont, and enjoys weaving the two genres together. He is a founding member of the bands Early Gray and Mount Rosco.

Megan Jean‚Äôs Secret Family is a fresh blend of the 70‚Äôs by way of the 90‚Äôs rock, soul, country, and jam, emphasizing vocal-driven danceable original songs that bring together generations of sound. In May of 2024, the band made their national radio debut on NPR‚Äôs Mountain Stage alongside notable acts like Keller Williams and Bettye Lavette. DC Music Review called them one of their ‚ÄúNew favorite acts‚Äù following an energitc performance at 2024‚Äôs DelFest. The band was awarded a creativity grant from the Maryland State Arts Council to produce their debut full-length album PLOT (Play.Loud.On.Turntable) which will be released in Fall 2024. In the three years since their formation they have shared the stage with Fastball, The Spin Doctors, Cabinet, Joan Osbourne, and Cris Jacobs.

Tom Hamilton‚Äôs guiding creative principle is very simple: he doesn‚Äôt like to repeat himself artistically.For the Philadelphia-based singer/songwriter andguitarist, this mindset stems from a deep-seated motivation to keep pushing himself as an artist ‚Äîto delve into different lyrical themes and musical detours, to explore potentially uncomfortable and unfamiliar emotional places.‚ÄùThat‚Äôs a thrilling prospect,‚Äù says Hamilton. ‚ÄúTo find the deeper things inside and to open up those doors that I didn‚Äôt even know were there.‚ÄùAs founding member of Brothers Past, American Babies, Joe Russo‚Äôs Almost Dead, Ghost Light and Billy and The Kids, Hamiltonhas written/recorded roughlya dozen albums and EPs and shared the stage with everyone from John Mayer to Bob Weir to Billy Strings.Hamilton‚Äôs debut record as a solo artist will be released in late 2025.

events.title: üéµ McKinney & MAMA
events.description:
Tonight, Sunday the 31st!
Doors at 7:30pm
Show at 8pm

McKinney is a bass player and singer-songwriter. She spent her formative years in southern West Virginia where she literally LIVED inside a music school. Deeply incapable of leaving the Appalachian mountains, but needing an escape from her 6,000 person hometown, she landed right around the bend in Asheville, NC.¬Ý

When McKinney isn‚Äôt on stage you might find her teaching music lessons at LEAF Global Arts, working shows at The Orange Peel, or at lock on the rugby pitch.¬Ý

McKinney‚Äôs music genre is about as fluid as the average Asheville resident‚Äôs sexuality. Her Indie Pop sound is blended with influence from her blues singer mother, a BFA in Jazz, and the 8 year old version of herself that just wants to play classic rock. Although pinning down her genre may be hard, you can consistently expect sad lyrics. You will probably enjoy her lyricism if you are emotionally suppressed and enjoy listening to sad songs to make yourself feel better.¬Ý¬Ý

MAMA is a Soul Shakin‚Äô Blues project from Asheville, NC. Fronted by Melissa McKinney, a vocalist known for her raw power, emotional delivery, and genuine connection with her audience, the band is quickly making a name for themselves in the blues world. MAMA earned Second Place at the International Blues Challenge this year in Memphis, TN. They also won the Merlefest Band Competition in 2025. Beyond their accolades, Melissa is a leader in the Asheville arts community. She serves as Programming Director for LEAF Global Arts, is the co-founder of the WTF (Women to the Front) Music Festival, and a board member of AVL Fest.

events.title: üéµ Tommy Prine with Brad Goodall
events.description:
Tonight, Saturday the 30th!
Doors at 7pm
Show at 8pm

Tommy Prine is a Nashville-bred songwriter carving out his own identity with unflinching honesty, dynamic guitar work, and a voice that bridges Americana, indie folk, and Southern storytelling. Following the breakout of his acclaimed debut album¬ÝThis Far South¬Ýand the momentum around his new EP¬ÝLove Circle¬Ý(out now), Prine has emerged as one of the most compelling young artists in the scene ‚Äî known for emotionally charged live shows, sharp songwriting, and a rapidly growing fanbase across the U.S. and Europe.

West Virginia songwriter and piano bard Brad Goodall established himself as a vivid storyteller with his 2022 debut Made In America, which boasted uproariously satirical character studies of sad, desperate men in the vein of Randy Newman and Warren Zevon. Where that album unfolded like a collection of biting short stories, his latest Hometown plays out like an autobiography..

events.description:
Tonight, Thursday the 28th!

Show at 8pm

Kevn Kinney is most widely known as founder and frontman of Atlanta-based rock band DrivinN-Cryin. A highly-acclaimed singer, songwriter, musician, performer, visual artist and poet, he¬Ý
has recently been inducted into both the Wisconsin Music Hall of Fame and Georgia Music Hall¬Ý
of Fame for his impactful legacy of art.
Kinney has released multiple critically acclaimed solo and band recordings during his career¬Ý
having also collaborated with Warren Haynes, REM, The Who and John Popper, among others.¬Ý
Peter Buck of REM has produced and performed on several of Kevn‚Äôs albums and continues to¬Ý
be involved in his career.
Kevn Kinney has continuously toured full-time with his band Drivin n Cryin while simultaneously¬Ý
carving out a niche for himself as a solo artist. From the festival stage to the smallest house¬Ý
concert, fans are always excited at the opportunity to see him up close and personal in a¬Ý
stripped-down acoustic setting.¬Ý
Recent touring include multiple dates with the Indigo Girls, Blackberry Smoke, The Black¬Ý
Crowes and others.
His latest solo studio album Think About It was released in 2022. In Fall 2023 the first release of¬Ý
the Let‚Äôs Go Dancin‚Äô: A Compilation Celebrating The Songs Of Kevn Kinney four-album vinyl¬Ý
series came out with over 100 digital recorded titles slated to go through end of 2024. The¬Ý
project is a massive all-star tribute to songwriter, poet, performer Kevn Kinney. Tracks from¬Ý
Jason Isbell, Violent Femmes, Shovels & Rope, Mike Farris, Great Peacock and dozens more¬Ý
will be released.

events.description:
Get ready to dive into the wild world of animal survival! Through hands-on science fun with microscopes, real specimens, stories, and games, children will uncover the fascinating ways creatures protect, hunt, and thrive. Scientist and author, Dawn Cusick, will share from her adventurous books "Animals That Make Me Say Ouch!" and "Animals That Make Me Say Look Out!".

This event introduces amazing animals ‚Äî from alligators and snakes to jellyfish, bats, and beetles through microscopes, games and specimens. Discover how each one uses its unique adaptations to stay one step ahead in nature‚Äôs game!

events.title: ü§ù Greensboro Public Library Anime Con
events.description:
Greensboro Public Library Anime Con is delighted to announce our next featured panelist!

Dr. Clair Nguyen serves as Lecturer in Music Theory at UNC Greensboro. Her primary research focuses on the intersections of music theory and film & media studies that includes films, music videos, video games, theme park music, Japanese anime, and Hatsune Miku rhythm games. Her secondary research area centers on Vietnamese popular and traditional music, including the v·ªçng c·ªï tradition.  She currently serves as advisor for the UNCG Animation Club as well as co-chair for the Society of Music Theory‚Äôs Film and Multimedia interest group.

She will be presenting her panel "The Intense Audiovisuals of Hatsune Miku," an exploration of how synchronized sounds and onscreen action create thoughtful and powerful storytelling in Vocaloid and Hatsune Miku: Colorful Stage! music videos.

Come meet Clair and join in on the fun on Saturday, August 8 from 10 am to 5 pm!

Eight visually rich vignettes drawn from Kurosawa‚Äôs own dreams‚Äîfox weddings and vanished orchards, a soldier‚Äôs ghosts, a walk through Van Gogh‚Äôs canvases, nuclear nightmares, and a water-mill utopia‚Äîmeditate on childhood, art, mortality, and humanity‚Äôs uneasy bond with nature.

Merry Christmas & Happy Holidays üéÑ As we approach the end of the year , we are thankful for all of the love and support we‚Äôve gotten thus far. It‚Äôs never easy starting something new, you‚Äôve gotta just put your best foot forward and go. But with God, the right people around you and a community-centric town like Greensboro, there‚Äôs no way to lose ‚ù§Ô∏è so I appreciate everyone who has shared a post, told a friend, or dragged a family member to one of our events. We‚Äôve done a lot in 9 months thanks to y‚Äôall, looking forward to what the next year brings! 

And since it‚Äôs the giving season we figured we‚Äôd give you the date of our next event üòÑ February 1st! More details and vendor information coming soon . Stay Tuned, Stay Warm, Stay Safe! ü´°

üì∏ | shottbyverde

events.title: ü§ù Trial of Alejandro Bodart
events.description:
Palestine solidarity is not a crime, solidarity with Alejandro Bodart. At the end of this month Argentine socialist, Alejandro Bodart will be on trial once again for his solidarity and support of the Palestinian people and their resistance to genocide. Bodart‚Äôs trial is not only about Bodart, it is an international trend to criminalize any expression of solidarity with Palestine, while the State of Israel has murdered over 180,000 Palestinians. Greensboro Revolutionary Socialists stand in solidarity with Bordart and all those across the world resisting genocide. #absolucionparabodart
 Group of revolutionary socialists in Greensboro, NC and members of Tempest Collective @tempest_mag

events.title: ü§ù Greensboro Revolutionary Socialists Special Meeting
events.description:
Greensboro Revolutionary Socialists would like to invite you to join us this Monday, February 10 at 7pm at 1333 Grove St for a special meeting, featuring a talk by organizer Kristen Godfrey on their article, "Borders are prisons and prisons are borders" with a discussion to follow.

*

"The way working-class people experience borders all over the world is tied to the theft of land and surplus value. Settler-colonialism‚Äôs life source is criminality and illegality, which is why we must be anti-capitalist abolitionists, developing a critical analysis of how imperialists‚Äô tactics work to divide us."

*

Tell us how this new context impacts you. Let's think about how we can resist together.
 Group of revolutionary socialists in Greensboro, NC and members of Tempest Collective @tempest_mag

events.title: üéµ Spotlight Record Launch Party - Latour b2b Jimmy D
events.description:
This Saturday we host the launch party of the new joint record from elsewhere‚Äôs two music directors Latour + Jimmy D, who‚Äôve teamed up to release ‚ÄúSpotlight‚Äù on the iconic Central Station Records. 

Latour b2b Jimmy D for a 3.5hr set this Saturday
with support from Rav & Cobella. 

About the record:
Latour and Jimmy D are two music directors on the Gold Coast at elsewhere who‚Äôve come together to write Spotlight. (their second collab)

Jimmy‚Äôs history dates back to being a vinyl DJ on the Sydney circuit in the 90‚Äôs, we wanted to bring back the flavour of that era with new updated NRG, that holds weight on today‚Äôs systems and events. 

The record being a standout memorable tune of the night with its stellar vocal which sees the majority of the record stripped away leaving a sassy ear catching moment in the rave. Then slowly creeping back up to an all our NRG finale. Think donk basslines, 90s rave synths & fast grooving raw drums.

A record to make SURE the dancefloor is indeed dancing. And if you aren‚Äôt then, ‚Äúgetttt the f offfffff ‚Äú

events.title: ü§ù Book Drive for Eastside Immigrant Community Center
events.description:
Throughout the month of April, Scuppernong will have a box at our register for this book drive for the youth program at Eastside Immigrant Community Center, a part of the Center for New North Carolinians at UNCG. cnnc.uncg ‚ÅÝ
‚ÅÝ
We're collecting grade school to YA books, with a comprehensive wishlist and guidelines at the EICC Book Drive link in our bio. Feel free to stop by the store to drop books in the box, or buy some on our website and leave us a note that it's for the drive! ‚ÅÝ
‚ÅÝ

‚ÅÝ

events.title: ü§ù TowneBank Summer Concert Series: The Shakedown
events.description:
Yesterday, we had the pleasure of celebrating the talented student artists behind this year‚Äôs Downtown Signal Box Student Art Project.

Surrounded by family, friends, and supporters, these young creatives were recognized for transforming ordinary signal boxes into vibrant works of public art that add color, creativity, and personality to downtown Greensboro.

Students received goodie bags and special maps highlighting the locations of signal boxes featuring student artwork, giving them the chance to explore downtown and see how their creativity is making a lasting impact on our community.

The celebration will continue this Thursday when artwork from several student participants is featured on the video board during the TowneBank Summer Concert Series at First National Bank Field as The Shakedown takes the stage.

To every student who shared their creativity with our community: thank you. Your artwork helps make downtown Greensboro a more vibrant, welcoming, and inspiring place for everyone. To the teachers who made this project possible, thank you for investing in your students and encouraging them to participate! 

Join us in congratulating these incredible young artists and enjoy a look back at yesterday‚Äôs reception through these photos.

Are you ready to celebrate Pride?!? Pride in our gender identity, gender expression, and sexual orientation happens year-round, and it's also great to have opportunities to celebrate together!

Join us at the Pyrle Theater in downtown Greensboro for a day of celebration on Saturday, June 20. The fun kicks off in the afternoon with family-friendly songs led by Mr. Sam of Scuppernong's Saturday Get-Downs, followed by a pizza dance party for youth and teens. To close out the night, the 21+ crowd will be treated to performances by drag superstars Thigh Fieri, Franny Starlight, and Brenda the Drag Queen, with DJ SmoothE providing tunes throughout the evening.

The Pyrle will have food and drinks available to keep the fun going. Tickets will be available soon through the Pyrle, so stay tuned!
 Advancing equality and inclusion through philanthropy, programming, and advocacy that uplifts the LGBTQ community.

events.title: üåà First Tuesdays with Friends Kickoff
events.description:
Join us for the KICKOFF of "First Tuesdays with Friends" TONIGHT, Tuesday April 7.  This is a new monthly social presented by GGF and Lewis & Elm that will be held on the first Tuesday of each month. Come enjoy some wine, cocktails, tasty non-alcoholic options, and delicious food in downtown Greensboro's Southend.
Lewis & Elm will be donating 15% of cocktail and mocktail sales to promote the vision of GGF to create a thriving LGBTQ community in the Triad.
This new monthly event will be in addition-to our Takeovers, where we will hop around the Triad.
The evening at Lewis & Elm is drop-in style between 5:30-7:30. Come when you can, leave when you must! See you tonight!
 Advancing equality and inclusion through philanthropy, programming, and advocacy that uplifts the LGBTQ community.

Today, two of the biggest new shows of the season took center stage, Schmigadoon and Ragtime. 
Schmigadoon, nominated for Best Musical, is the hilarious show about a couple who accidentally end up in a magical town where every day is a musical.
Ragtime, nominated for Best Revival of a Musical, is an epic telling of the American dream.
We‚Äôre so excited to see what the future holds tomorrow at the Tony Awards for these two great new shows and on the road as they both begin national tours next fall.

events.title: üé® North Carolinians‚Äô Fight for Independence: The Soldier‚Äôs Experience
events.location: Main Hall of East Building, NCMA
events.description:
The State Archives of North Carolina (nc_archives_photos) are bringing their "One-Day Wonder" pop-up exhibition to the NCMA this Friday, June 12, from 10 am-4 pm. In commemoration of America250NC, the State Archives created a traveling exhibit to explore the experiences of North Carolina soldiers fighting in the Revolutionary War.

Titled "North Carolinians‚Äô Fight for Independence: The Soldier‚Äôs Experience," this exhibit will provide the public with an opportunity to explore the topic through original records, rare reproductions, and informational exhibit panels.

As the name indicates, this is a one-day-only event. These historic pieces will be on view in the Main Hall of East Building.

You can learn more about America 250 at the State Archives at the link in our bio.

---------
Pictured: Pay Voucher for Edward Going, No Date
Treasurer‚Äôs and Comptroller‚Äôs Papers, Military Papers, Revolutionary Pay Vouchers, 1779-1782.

A pay voucher addressed to Edward Going for six pounds, twelve shillings, and four pence. The date is missing due to hole punches.

events.title: üé® Teen Night 2026: Around the Campfire
events.description:
The week is finally here - our free Knowing the West-themed "Teen Night 2026: Around the Campfire" is this Friday, June 12, from 5:30 pm-8:30 pm!

We put together a walkthrough so you (or your teen) will know where to go and what to expect when you arrive at East Building.

We have a ton of fun stuff planned, so please register for free at the link in our bio! 

Please note that this event is for teens ages 13-18.

events.title: üé® NCMA Loves Anime Bazaar
events.description:
When shy virtual reality moderator Kenji agrees to pose as the pretty and popular Natsuki's affluent fake boyfriend, a rogue AI steals his identity and hacks the virtual reality system. Accused of causing real-world catastrophes, he must unite Natsuki's family to stop an impending cyber apocalypse.

On Saturday, June 13, we are not only hosting our biggest NCMA Loves Anime Bazaar yet, but afterward, the Triangle Choir Club will perform anime songs, followed by an 8 pm screening of Mamoru Hosoda's "Summer Wars" (summary above).

Both of these events are included with your ticket for the film screening, so make sure to stick around for more anime fun!

Get your ticket at the link in our bio.

---------
Image: Courtesy of GKIDS

events.title: üé® Always Looking: Titus Brooks Heagins
events.description:
Reminder: Join us on Friday evening, June 5, for a screening of the 2025 documentary, "Always Looking: Titus Brooks Heagins."

This film looks at the work of photographer Titus Brooks Heagins, whose photos document those marginalized by race, gender/sexual identity, and poverty. The film also explores his own challenges of capturing these stories while posing the question, "who can tell whose story?"

Get your tickets at the link in our bio.

events.title: üé® Still. Moving free dance performance by Frankie Lee III
events.description:
The next "Still. Moving" free dance performance at the NCMA will be this Saturday, June 6, at 11 am and 1 pm in the Museum Park. Join us as Frankie Lee III (fleedance) presents a new work inspired by Wim Botha's sculptures, "Prism 5" and "Prism 7."

Botha‚Äôs sculptures explore weighty questions of history, status, power, and religion. Suspended wings hover above the ground, lifted‚Äîand restrained‚Äîby thin rectangular supports that seem to both elevate and tether them.

Are these the wings of unseen, silent angels? Or do they belong to something darker? Through movement, Lee and the dancers enter that tension between ascension and gravity, freedom and control.

--------
Images 1 and 3: Wim Botha, Prism 5 (2013) and Prism 7 (2014), bronze and stainless steel, dimensions variable, Purchased with funds from the Modern and Contemporary Deaccession Fund
Image 2: Frankie Lee III, courtesy of the artist

events.title: üé® NCMA Dance Gumbo
events.description:
Sure, it's Monday, but let's look ahead to this Saturday - if you want to get your weekend started with a good workout, come on out to the Museum Park at 9 am on June 6 for NCMA Dance Gumbo!

Led by instructor Jordan Farrell, this class blends pop, Zumba, Latin, hip-hop, and more into an energetic, all-levels aerobic workout that celebrates movement, music, and culture.

Get your free tickets at the link in our bio.

----------
Photo: Courtesy of the instructor

events.title: üé® Please Be Seated - North Carolina Official Public Opening with Dance Activation
events.description:
The fencing around the Museum Park's latest installation, "Please Be Seated - North Carolina," created by PaulCocksedge, is coming down! We'll be celebrating the official public opening tomorrow, Tuesday, June 2, with a special dance activation.

Directed by choreographers Christa Oliver and Betsy Graves, the performance features live musicians alongside over 40 dancers from ThePanoramicDanceProject (NCSU), Broughton Dance (CapsDance), and communities across North Carolina.

The performance begins at 6:30 pm and is free to attend. The dance should last about 15 minutes, so make sure to get there on time!

Click the link in our bio for more details.

events.title: üé® Please Be Seated - North Carolina Opening and Dance Performance
events.description:
Exciting news! The NCMA's latest outdoor installation, "Please Be Seated - North Carolina," created by PaulCocksedge, will open to the public in just a few days - on Tuesday, June 2.

To celebrate, we are hosting a special dance performance at 6:30 pm sharp featuring live musicians and dozens of dancers from ThePanoramicDanceProject, Broughton Dance (CapsDance), and communities across the state.

Join us for a vibrant celebration of movement, music, and public art!

Click the link in our bio for all the details.

events.title: üé® Still. Moving: Zonal Harmonic 170,000 and Zonal Harmonic 220,000
events.description:
Interpreted by Rainbow Dance Company Director of Dance Kathryn Auman, this Saturday's free "Still. Moving: Zonal Harmonic 170,000 and Zonal Harmonic 220,000" performance will explore the unseen architectures that shape how we move, gather, and belong.

Inspired by Tom√°s Saraceno‚Äôs vision of airborne networks and interspecies connection, Rainbow Dance/ArtsTogether will perform a piece that considers the body not as an isolated figure but as part of a larger, vibrating system‚Äîheld in tension, resonance, and relation.

A brief talkback with Auman and the dancers follows the performance.

Join us in West Building at either 11 am or 1 pm on May 30 to see art in motion - and watch the video to see clips of Rainbow Dance/Arts Together's previous Still. Moving interpretation of Yoan Capote's "Isla (Tierra Prometida)."

events.title: üé® From Irving Berlin to Louis Armstrong
events.description:
Tickets are running low for Thursday evening's concert, "From Irving Berlin to Louis Armstrong," featuring renowned internationally touring jazz pianist JoeAlterman and his quartet, plus special guest ChristianTamburrProductions!

This concert celebrates the extraordinary creative exchange between Jewish composers and African American performers that helped define the sound of the 20th century. Join us at 7 pm on May 28 as these musicians explore iconic works by legendary figures such as Irving Berlin, George Gershwin, Duke Ellington, and Louis Armstrong.

Get your tickets at the link in our bio before they sell out!

events.title: üé® Hildaland
events.description:
This weekend! Don‚Äôt miss two incredible shows at The ArtsCenter. 

Firstly, on Thursday night don‚Äôt miss the chance to see New England duo Hildaland as they continue their tour through the southeast! 

A contemporary collage of Scottish and American traditional music, Hildaland is a duo that approaches geographic borders as markers of exploration rather than boundaries to be wary of. Fiddle player Louise Bichan, originally from Orkney, Scotland, and mandolinist Ethan Setiawan, originally from Goshen, Indiana, have taken their duo from traditional Scottish roots through to progressive bluegrass and jazz, with music inspired largely by places and the journeys between them, both physical and musical. 

Then on Saturday, Pop Up Chorus returns with a special Broadway edition centering the songs from the smash hit musical Hamilton, which is celebrating its 11th year with a national tour now playing at DPAC! Come sing songs from the show with PopUp Broadway here at The ArtsCenter on June 6!

Tickets on sale at artscenterlive.org

events.title: üé® Horse Lords
events.description:
JUST ANNOUNCED: SUNDAY 10/11 8:00 PM ‚Äî Horse Lords (local support TBD) 

Horse Lords were founded in Baltimore, Maryland in 2010; they evolved from the experimental collective Teeth Mountain and began as a trio with drummer Sam Haberman, guitarist Owen Gardner, and bassist Max Eilbacher, soon adding alto saxophonist Andrew Bernstein to the core ensemble. Though Horse Lords grew out of the fertile Baltimore noise and leftfield rock scene, a storied environment for artists and weirdos that has nurtured many an influential outsider band (Lungfish, Matmos), their approach is more omnivorous than the stippled rhythms of instrumental electric rock would indicate. 

Across sixteen years of frequent touring, Horse Lords have released ten studio, live, and collaboration albums in addition to a split EP with fellow Baltimoreans Lower Dens, a handful of singles and compilation tracks. For RVNG‚Äôs FRKWYS series, the band collaborated with Berlin-based composer Arnold Dreyblatt, a onetime student of La Monte Young and Pauline Oliveros whose just intonation chamber music was a crucial part of the post-Fluxus Downtown New York City environment. 

With the relocation of three of the four members to Germany beginning in 2021, the potential for more further creative exploration, and performance opportunities including the Moers Festival (with an 11-piece lineup, interpreting Julius Eastman‚Äôs music as well as their own), the interdisciplinary phases of the band‚Äôs music was poised for fascinating new directions. The band observes ‚ÄúWe are always trying to balance working within the limitations of the quartet and pushing beyond them.

For their second studio album on RVNG Intl., Demand To Be Taken To Heaven Alive!, Horse Lords have followed this notion and augmented the instrumentation, expanding the format slightly to include bass clarinetist Madison Greenstone (TAK Ensemble), trombonist Weston Olencki (Nate Wooley, RAGE Thormbones) and vocalists Nina Guo (Departure Duo) and Evelyn Saylor (Holly Herndon, Caterina Barbieri) across a program of twelve original compositions. 

Read more/buy tickets at https://artscenterlive.org/performances-events

events.title: üéµ An Evening with thegalentines.band
events.description:
Happy to announce An Evening with thegalentines.band in  Gas Hill Drinking Room on July 8, 2026! Doors at 7 PM, show at 8 PM.

Tickets are on sale NOW at TheRamkat.com! 

The Galentines are an all-girl indie-rock band who are the very heart of the local music scene in Boulder, Colorado. They are known for their stunning harmonies blended into epic instrumental solos that remind the listener of classic rock bands such as Fleetwood Mac and Pink Floyd, while maintaining their punk edge with biting lyricism. Their music ‚Äúcelebrates the complexity of womanhood, capturing all of its challenges and triumphs‚Äù (Medium), while breaking the barriers that define the female experience. Encompassing multiple genres, including indie, punk and rock, the girls just released their heavily anticipated single Eden, which is now available on all streaming platforms. #thegalentines #gashilldrinkingroom #wsnc #supportlivemusic

events.title: üéµ Nether Hour
events.description:
Happy to welcome netherhour  in from Austin, Texas to Gas Hill Drinking Room on June 24, 2026! Doors at 7 PM, show at 8 PM.

Tickets are on sale NOW at TheRamkat.com!

‚ÄúWe don‚Äôt play country music, but we‚Äôre good ole boys from the country.‚Äù

Down in the heart of Austin, TX, where the music runs thicker than the heat, there‚Äôs a band that‚Äôs been making some serious noise. They call themselves Nether Hour, and they‚Äôve been turning every dive bar and backroad juke joint into a full-blown musical revival. The band is a trio of unapologetic rebels, each one a powerhouse of sonic defiance. Bobby Flaco delivers gritty vocals and razor-sharp guitar riffs, while Bishop unleashes searing lead guitar and mandolin work, with Bercy laying down bass lines that slam right into your gut. Nether Hour is a relentless sonic melting pot, fusing the swampy grooves of Louisiana with Texas-sized doses of funk, blues, soul, and rock ‚Äòn‚Äô roll. #netherhour #gashilldrinkingroom #wsnc #supportlivemusic

events.title: üéµ Fruit Bats with alolender
events.description:
Excited  to welcome alolender to The Ramkat on July 16, 2026 in support of Fruit Bats! 

Get your tickets NOW at TheRamkat.com!

Songwriter Al Olender tries to grasp the brief, beautiful moments that make up our lives in slow-motion but finds that the tighter she holds on, the more these frames fall into a soupy blur. Change is our only constant, but what if we wanted to steep in the now? What if we could stay right here? Olender captures the anxieties of an ever-moving world with a sweet and silly sincerity on her second full-length album The Worrier, never shying away from staring the truth right in the face. Exploring anxiety, sexuality and anarchy, both in herself and in her relationships, Olender‚Äôs acoustic, bluesy melodies point to the butterflies of first crushes, the gut-punching realities of responsibility and the eternal romance of friendship. #alolender #fruitbats #theramkat #wsnc #supportlivemusic

events.title: üé® ADWS Annual Meeting
events.description:
ART CRUSH is evolving, and we‚Äôre doing it with intention.

This year, we‚Äôre focusing on fewer, more thoughtfully planned events to create stronger experiences for artists, attendees, and the businesses that make the Arts District of Winston-Salem thrive.

Here‚Äôs what 2026 looks like:
üåà May ‚Äì Color the District
üèôÔ∏èJuly ‚Äì Winston-Salem Weekend
üéâ September ‚Äì Hispanic Heritage Month
üõçÔ∏è November ‚Äì Arts District Celebration

Each event is designed to feel meaningful, vibrant, and well-supported, with clearer themes, deeper engagement, and more space to do things really well.

What‚Äôs new for artists:
We‚Äôre introducing a $25 participation fee with 35 paid artist spots per event, plus 5 scholarship-funded spots for emerging artists. This helps sustain ART CRUSH while keeping access and opportunity at the center of what we do.

One more shift:
We‚Äôre moving away from annual memberships. Artists can now participate event by event: simple, flexible, and clear. (Full details coming to our website soon!)

Want the full story?
Join us for the ADWS Annual Meeting at chadschai 
üìÖ February 27 | ‚è∞ 6:00‚Äì7:30 PM

Come curious. Bring a friend who‚Äôs new to ART CRUSH. Stay for the conversation.‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã‚Äã

events.title: üé® Acting Through Song Masterclass with Kerry Butler
events.description:
Fresh off their Junior Theatre Festival run, look what our rising sTArs have coming up next! ta.rising.stars 

‚ú® An unforgettable experience is coming to Winston-Salem! ‚ú®

üé≠ Tony Award-nominated Broadway star Kerry Butler is headlining an exclusive Acting Through Song Masterclass & Rising sTArs Broadway Cabaret hosted by Theatre Alliance Rising sTArs! üåü

Kerry Butler has graced the Broadway stage originating roles in Beetlejuice, Mean Girls, Xanadu, Little Shop of Horrors, Catch Me If You Can, Hairspray, and so many more! üåü From Broadway to TV, she‚Äôs done it all! üì∫

üé§ In this masterclass, eight performers will work one-on-one with Kerry and all attendees will receive invaluable tips on audition techniques, character development, and vocal performance. PLUS, there will be a Q&A! üôå

üé∂ After the class, join Kerry and local performers for a one-night-only cabaret event featuring show-stopping performances and behind-the-scenes stories from the Broadway world. üé≠‚ú®

Want to sing on stage with Kerry? üé§ Registration for the masterclass includes a ticket to the cabaret and the chance to perform LIVE! üé∂üí•

üìÖ Masterclass: March 8th, 2026 | 1-5 PM
üìç Location: Juxtaposition Studios, Winston-Salem, NC
üéüÔ∏è Registration Fee: $75 | Register at link in comments 

üåü Cabaret: March 8th, 2026 | 8 PM
üìç Location: Winston-Salem Theatre Alliance
üéüÔ∏è Tickets: $25 | Get yours here:  https://theatrealliance.ws/box-office/

Don‚Äôt miss your chance to learn from one of Broadway‚Äôs brightest stars! ‚ú®

events.description:

‚ÄúThe Rocky Horror Show‚Äù-LIVE! (9th Annual Production)

October 24, 25, 26, 29, 30, and November 1 at 7:30 PM‚Äî Nightly costume contest for $50 Village Tavern gift card!

$25/$23 for ‚ÄúAlien: The Musical Parody‚Äù

events.title: üé® This Side of Crazy
events.location: The Beall Theatre at Theatre Alliance - 651 Pilot View St NW Winston Salem, NC 27101
events.description:
Don‚Äôt be an outsider looking in! Enjoy a matinee with us this Saturday!

‚ÄúThis Side of Crazy,‚Äù by Del Shores, is next in Theatre Alliance‚Äôs Beall Theatre! When three feuding sisters are summoned home by their popular gospel-music composing mother to perform one final musical concert, old tensions flare, and the outcome is anything but blessed and holy! Grab a front-row seat to the latest by the southern writer who brought us ‚ÄúSordid Lives,‚Äù ‚ÄúSouthern Baptist Sissies,‚Äù and ‚ÄúThe Trials and Tribulations of a Trailer Trash Housewife!‚Äù

September 20 and 27 at 12 PM and 3 PM

Tickets: $15

Get them here: https://theatrealliance.ws/box-office/

Rated R for language, sexual content, mature subject matter

LOCATION: The Beall Theatre at Theatre Alliance - 651 Pilot View St NW Winston Salem, NC 27101

Signature Drink: The Sandi Patti

Running Time: 2 hours, with one 15-minute Intermission

(same physical building as Ihrie Theatre, but the entrance is on Pilot Street- free parking in the upper lot adjacent to the building. (The Centenary lot beside our building.))

Pictured: Katy Carroll

Photos: Neil Jester - Fine Art Photography

events.title: üé® Hairspray
events.location: Winston-Salem Theatre Alliance‚Äôs Jamie Lawson Auditorium, 650 W Sixth Ave, Winston-Salem, NC
events.description:
Back to Baltimore tonight for ‚ÄúHairspray!‚Äù Tonight‚Äôs forecast for rain presents an ideal opportunity to stay dry and enjoy an evening with a multi-Tony-Award-winning musical!

‚ÄúHairspray‚Äù bursts onto the Theatre Alliance stage in September!  If you aren‚Äôt familiar with this colorful confection of a show, you should be!  Follow 1960s teeny-bopper Tracy Turnblad and her friends and family as they navigate the fight for civil rights and everyone‚Äôs right to dance, because ‚ÄúYou Can‚Äôt Stop The Beat!‚Äù Arguably featuring one of the best, high-energy scores in several decades, ‚ÄúHairspray‚Äù is a show that celebrates the best in all of us!

Performing these dates at Winston-Salem Theatre Alliance‚Äôs Jamie Lawson Auditorium, 650 W Sixth Ave, Winston-Salem, NC:

September 12-13, 19-20 at 7:30 PM
September 14 and 21 at 2 PM
ADDED: September 26 and 27 at 7:30 PM

Free parking onsite!

Tickets: https://theatrealliance.ws/box-office/

Pricing: $25/$23

Rated PG-13 for innuendo, mild language 

Run time: 2 hours, 15 mins with one Intermission 

Signature Drink: Edna‚Äôs Elixir

Season Sponsors:

events.title: üé® The Shark is Broken
events.location: Beall Theatre at Theatre Alliance, 651 Pilot View St, Winston-Salem, NC
events.description:
Break a Leg to our Cast and Crew of ‚ÄúThe Shark is Broken,‚Äù opening this afternoon in the Beall Theatre at 3 PM and also playing Sunday at 5:30 PM! (Missing from photo are Jeremy Engel, Alex Fredwall, and John Wilson).

The first summer blockbuster movie is being filmed‚Äîbut no one working on the film would know it. Dive deep into the tumultuous, murky waters of the making of a major motion picture with testy, feuding costars, unpredictable weather, and a shark prop whose constant breakdowns are looking like an omen for the future of the movie. In this comedy co-written by Ian Shaw and Joseph Nixon, the short tempers of Jaws stars Robert Shaw (father of co-writer Ian), Richard Dreyfuss, and Roy Scheider take center stage as they bond, argue, drink, gamble, and pray for an end to the shoot, not knowing it will change their lives forever

Make a double-feature of It and catch ‚ÄúThe Prince of Egypt,‚Äù also playing this weekend!

The Beall Theatre at Theatre Alliance (upper level, upper parking lot), 651 Pilot View St, Winston-Salem, NC

Saturday, Aug 16 at 3 PM
Sunday, Aug 17 at 5:30 PM
Saturday, Aug 23 at 3 PM and 5:30 PM

Photos: Neil Jester - Fine Art Photography

Tickets: https://theatrealliance.ws/box-office/dl

events.description:
Come out and celebrate 2 years of WVM!
Tomorrow Saturday April 4th from 12-5pm

üìç486 N Patterson Ave, Winston Salem, NC
Incendiary Brewing at Bailey Park

üíôTips and Information:

‚Ä¢We have 25+ vintage clothing and accessories vendors !
Jewelry by honestjewelers 

‚Ä¢Our vendors will have all sorts of styles and price points. You will find anything from a $10 rack to premium vintage goods!

‚Ä¢We are giving away new WVM totes to the first 50 customers!

‚≠êÔ∏èHave you shopped with us before? If not here are some tips:

1) Each booth is its own separate entity. Make sure to check out with that vendor before walking away

2) Bring a reusable bag to carry your purchases. Our goal is to limit waste.

3) Shop early and don‚Äôt hesitate! Vintage items are typically 1 of 1. It is more than likely you will never see that item again!

4) Vendors accept Venmo, Zelle, Cashapp, Cash, and/or Tap to Pay
  Each vendor is different so make sure to ask what form is best. Cash makes things easier !

That‚Äôs pretty much it! I hope this helps in planning your trip tomorrow! There are tons of food options and coffee in the area! 

We will see you there!

What is WVM:
We are a monthly vintage and secondhand clothing market. Our vendors curate tons of secondhand items every month; items like carhartt and nascar jackets, coach purses, belts, jewelry, shoes, decor, and so much more. 
Our goal is to bring new people into the secondhand world! By doing so we save tons of articles from textile/land waste which in turn helps our environment!

ABCDEFGHIJ ‚ÄúK‚Äù for Kids this Alphabet Movie Night we will be gathering at the lGBTQ Center of Durham Warehouse on Hunt for a Kid & Family Film. #family #lgbtq #queer #kidsmovie #familyfun #community #pajamaparty #popcorn

events.title: üåà Get Shit Done Evening
events.description:
Some of the biggest barriers to survivor safety are finance related. While we do our best to put cash directly into survivors hands, in tandem, we thought it would be awesome to create a body doubling/coworking space where we can help each other executive function. As well as offer relevant financial resources! That‚Äôs why we are launching ‚ÄúGet Shit Done‚Äù evenings. They will occur biweekly and be available in person or online (email advocate@lgbtqcenterofdurham.org for the virtual link). If you or someone you know is a money expert who wants to share their knowledge with queer/trans survivors, please reach out!

Image Descriptions/Alt Text:
Slide 1: Background is a wood grain table but in a lavender and dark purple hue. On top of the table, acting as the base of the image, is an open spiral notebook. The metal spirals are gold and the paper is white and lined. There is a faded coffee mug ring on the paper. In the top right corner of the image is a crumbled up piece of paper. On the right side of the image is a black pen. At the bottom right corner of the image is a folded set of marble colored glasses. On the spiral notebook in a black pen-like handwriting font that reads ‚Äú‚ÄúGet Shit Done, Every Other Tuesday‚Äù. Below that reads ‚Äú(bolded) 5:30-7p, 114 Hunt St‚Äù all highlighted in purple. Below that it reads, ‚ÄúIn person & Virtual Options‚Äú (underlined)‚Äù With purple and pink stars on the right of it. Below that reads ‚ÄúA virtual space to do the $$ things we‚Äôve all been avoiding. Come work on taxes, budgeting, filling out that form... whatever you need!‚Äù At the bottom of the notebook page is the SQSN mushroom logo and Southern Queer Survivor Network in all purple bold+caps font.

 #survivors #lgbtq #durhamnc #economicjustice #queer #trans

Please note the Center hours vary daily. Our event calendar on our website will be updated with our hours at the beginning of each week. Please call ahead if you plan to visit 919-827-1436.

Tuesday May 26: Family Art Party & Community Listening Session (118 Hunt) 5-7 registration required: bit.ly/artpartydurham

Trans POC Support Group (114 Hunt St) 5-7
Virtual Know Your Rights: Finding a Therapist, 7-8:30

Thursday May 28: STI/HIV testing (114 Hunt St) 1-3PM

Friday May 29: Front Porch Friday (114 Hunt St) 12-1

Saturday May 30: Kids Movie Nite - Into the Spiderverse, (118 Hunt Street) 6:30-8

events.title: üåà Monthly POC Trans Support Group
events.description: If you're a person of color Trans or gender nonconforming community member, join us for our Monthly POC Trans Support Group to discuss your trans experience and normalized joy in your life. The support group meets the last Wednesday of each month from 5 to 7 PM.

events.title: üåà Welcome in Wednesday: Flower Pressing/Pet Rock
events.location: LGBTQ Center of Durham, 114 Hunt St, Durham
events.description:
Put Some Play in your Day!

Welcome in Wednesday with Stormie Daie, meetups for youth 5-12, and families held at the LGBTQ Center of Durham at 114 Hunt St and participating community spaces.  This is your chance to engage the children in the LGBTQ Durham community by sharing space and activities together. Welcome in Wednesday will prioritize fun through social gathering and activities emphasizing nature, science, sharing, education, reading, and hands on crafts.

This time will serve to create connections amongst youth and families and strengthen community ties with and amongst younger lgbtq folks and their families.

As well we will inform the children and families with the happenings of the LGBTQ Center through partnerships with likeminded groups and community partners.

For questions, concerns, registration or interest in sponsoring/supporting Welcome in Wednesday, contact Stormie Daie at sdaie@lgbtqcenterofdurham.org

4/15 -  Flower Pressing/Pet Rock
4/22 -  Terrarium
4/29 - Tea Party
5/6 - Kiteing
5/13 - Face Paint
5/20 - Dress Up Tea Party
5/22 - Show and Tell Talent Show

*activities are subject to change due to weather and space/supply needs

This is not child care, parents or guardians or care takers are expected to be present.

events.title: üåà Alphabet Movie Nite: K for the Kids - Space Jam
events.description:
Let's get reeling and rolling for our first ‚ÄúAlphabet Movie Nite‚Äù here at the Queerhouse Theater. This first screening is ‚ÄúK‚Äù for the Kids with all ages welcome! 

We will be watching the 90s multimedia sports comedy animated classic ‚ÄúSpace Jam,‚Äù starring NC‚Äôs very own, plus all your favorite cartoon athletes - and hosted by Durham's own, Stormie Daie! 

Screening is at May 9 at 6:30pm with a runtime of 1:30min. Space is ample but seating is limited so please bring a blanket or lawn chair if possible. 

Also feel free to bring your best stuffie and wear your best pjs so you can crawl home and get in bed promptly after the finish of the film! 

Popcorn provided. Feel free to bring outside meals/snacks for you and yours.

Come early - parking is limited, primarily street parking.

events.title: üåà Black Trans Short Film Festival
events.description:
We‚Äôre proud to partner with Comfrey Films for the Black Trans Short Film Festival‚Äîan inspiring celebration of bold, culture-shifting films by Black trans, gender non-conforming, and intersex filmmakers.
Register for free to join us at the Black Trans Short Film Festival in Durham, NC April 30-May 2nd. comfreyfilms.org/festival 

Can‚Äôt make it to Durham this month? Get your ticket to BTSFF virtual! 
This year‚Äôs theme, ‚ÄúWe are free now,‚Äù centers storytelling that does not ask for permission‚Äîstories that are shaping culture, building community, and imagining new possibilities for the future.
The Black Trans Short Film Festival features a dynamic lineup of film screenings, filmmaker conversations, and community events that celebrate emerging and established voices in Black trans media.
Join us in supporting these incredible artists and experiencing the power of their work.
üé¨ Learn more and get tickets: comfreyfilms.org/festival
We hope to see you there!

events.title: üåà Comfrey Films Black Trans Short Film Festival
events.description:
TDOV RECAP

Let's Reminisce on our Trans Day of Visibility Weekend last month! This was a partnership between BLMP SONG TLC and The LGBTQ Center of Durham. The weekend started with a Trans Short Film Screening in partnership with Comfrey Films and concluded with our Annual TDOV Tea. 

Don't forget to check out the Comfrey Films Black Trans Short Film Festival this weekend! 

officialblmp ignitekindred comfreyfilms translawcenter

events.title: ü§ù Get Out of My Dreams: June Pride Edition
events.description:
So remember when Arcana turned TEN in December, and Erin threw the dance party of her dreams with four of her favorite DJs? Well this is a party that just won‚Äôt stop- we did it again in March, and now we‚Äôre BACK for our June Pride edition with Fifi Hifi and VSPRTN. Get Out of My Dreams, get into my car, and let‚Äôs road trip through time together! A  four hour dance party replete with all the sapphic longing and yearning and fantasizing we can fit into one night. Big sapphic dance hits, divas, and whatever else Fifi and Ves are feeling for this very special Pride party. Robyn, Prince, Florence, Whitney, Kylie, Prince, Janelle Monae, FKA Twigs, Gaga, Sia, Charli, and beyond.

Last December and March this party was in full swing by 10pm, so do arrive early!

This show will sell out! Get your $10 tickets in advance- link in bio.

 21+ with proper ID

events.title: ü§ù Mending Workshop with River Takada
events.description:
TOMORROW! ü™°

River Takada (rivtak) (she/they) will be walking around helping trouble shoot your mending projects, and words of encouragement and tops of how to practically mend your texts. 

At 7:15 they‚Äôll be doing a beginner hand sewing demo! 

Needles, thread, random fabric scraps, and scissors will be provided.

Bring 1-2 textile (clothing, bag, patching) mending projects you would like to ask questions about and get started on! 

NO hemming and alteration projects please! 

BYO zippers, buttons, matching thread or fabric for patching, etc. 

It‚Äôs still craft night, so other craft projects are welcome to come and mingle with the sewing! 

(Free event but tips for River are always welcome!)üí∏

events.title: ü§ù Forever Barbara screening
events.location: Full Frame Documentary Film Festival, Durham
events.description:
Arcana is proud sponsors of the Full Frame Documentary Film Festival happening this weekend in Durham!! 

There are some great documentaries happening this weekend, but we‚Äôre especially excited for Friday‚Äôs screening of Forever Barbara: 

Feminist icon and lesbian filmmaker Barbara Hammer‚Äôs life mission was to create and record lesbian histories, both personal and societal. With Hammer‚Äôs own voice as our guide, Barbara Forever weaves elements from her personal archive‚Äîfilms, home movies, photos, audio recordings‚Äîand interviews with her longtime partner, Florrie Burke, to create an expansive portrait of its subject. 

playing at 7:30! tickets at fullframefest !!! 

Stop in before or after the screening for a springtime cocktail! If you pop in after, you might catch Ally J and Kevin Clark‚Äôs set!!

events.title: üåà TRAMP Dance Party
events.description:
GAG! is launching a dance party on January 9th! TRAMP was created in response to the community for a dance party ‚Äî a raw, no disruptions, nonstop music dance party where you can dance freely. Once Bitter Social opened, a new downtown bar by the makers of Bitter Sweet and Johnson Street Yacht Club, it became clear this space was made for just that!

This small intimate space creates a different vibe from any GAG party that promotes getting closer with each other; whether it‚Äôs dancing or conversing in one their dark corners.
GAG is bringing two special guest DJs, featuring, Pisces Prince (Gaven Simon) from NYC ‚Äî featured in So.Gay in 2025 and GHO3 SPINZ from GAG to Death this past Halloween, will pump up the BPMs FOR EVERYONE tramping on the dance floor!

Not only is it a premiere of a new party ‚Äî but it serves as a birthday celebration of GAG producer, Trey Roberts!

Tickets at durhamfruit.com

events.title: üåà Pride Speed Dating & Screening: It's Not You. It's Me: Chicago!
events.description:
üé• Celebrate Pride with connection and community!

Join Out! Raleigh Pride and the LGBT Center of Raleigh for a night of speed dating, speed friending, or just connecting.. followed by the Raleigh premiere screening of It's Not You. It's Me: Chicago!

This award-winning queer dating and mental health docuseries is making international waves and now it‚Äôs coming to Raleigh for one night only! Bring your boo, your bestie, or both! You don't want to miss this! Proceeds support the completion of the series and continued services at the Center ü©∑ . Grab your ticket using the link in our bio! 

*speed dating/friending not required!

events.title: üåà ASPYRE Youth Leadership Camp Registration Opens
events.description:
üö® The countdown is on! üö®

Registration for ASPYRE Youth Leadership Camp opens TOMORROW, June 1!

ASPYRE Youth Leadership Camp empowers young members of the LGBTQ+ community and their allies to achieve personal growth through activism and community engagement. Camp ASPYRE provides our youth an opportunity to discover their authentic voices while strengthening their leadership and advocacy skills.. all while connecting with other passionate young people who are also ready to create change.

Open to youth ages 13 to 18 who are currently enrolled in high school.

Mark your calendars and spread the word!

Registration opens tomorrow, June 1! üåà‚ú® Keep an eye out for links and additional information

Questions? Contact aspyre@lgbtcenterofraleigh.com.

events.title: üåà Camp ASPYRE 2026 Registration Opens
events.description:
Registration for Camp ASPYRE 2026 opens in exactly ONE WEEK on June 1st!! Heart + share this if you're as excited as we are!

ASPYRE Youth Leadership Camp is a powerful experience designed to empower young members of the LGBTQ community and their allies to achieve personal growth through advocacy, activism, and leadership development. Campers will build confidence, learn how to organize and advocate for their communities, and connect with other young people who are passionate about creating positive change.

ASPYRE Youth Leadership Camp welcomes youth ages 13 to 18 who are still in high school and are ready to step into leadership and make a meaningful difference in their communities.

Have questions? Reach out to the ASPYRE team at aspyre@lgbtcenterofraleigh.com for more information.

#CampASPYRE #ASPYRE2026

events.title: ü§ù The Wiley Fosters - Last Show at Mystery Brewing Company
events.description:
Today, we‚Äôre sharing sad news. After almost seven years, Mystery Brewing Company will be closing its doors at the end of the day on October 31st.
.
.
This has been an incredibly difficult decision. We‚Äôve always been undercapitalized, and it‚Äôs been a struggle to operate for some time now. We‚Äôve suffered a string of pretty bad luck over the past couple of years: equipment failures, construction and permitting delays, storm-related outages and losses. The end result is that we can no longer afford to operate.
.
.
Over the course of the next week, we‚Äôll be doing the best we can to celebrate everything we love about Mystery to give it a fond farewell, releasing some favorite old beers that we‚Äôve had kicking around in the back of the cooler, and trying to say goodbye the best way we can. If you get a chance, please come and visit us, say hi, and enjoy a pint, a meal, or a night of music for the last time. The Wiley Fosters ‚Äì our amazing house band ‚Äì will be playing our last show at the pub on the evening of October 27th.
.
.
We‚Äôre enormously proud of the community that has grown up around Mystery, and we‚Äôll miss being here every day for Hillsborough.
.
.
With one last raised glass,
.
.
Erik Lars Myers
Founder, Brewer, CEO
Mystery Brewing Company

events.title: ü§ù An Unlikely Lent: Extraordinary People of the Easter Story
events.description:
Whether you‚Äôve observed Lent for years or you still have questions about what it even is, we invite you to join us and learn together!

We‚Äôre intentionally journeying through Lent in our next discipleship course, An Unlikely Lent: Extraordinary People of the Easter Story, starting TONIGHT, Feb. 24 at 7 p.m. Together, we‚Äôll explore the surprising, ordinary, and unlikely people woven into the Easter story and what we can learn from each of them. 

üìÜ Tuesdays at 7 p.m. at The Foundry

Bring your curiosity, your experiences, and your questions!

events.title: üéµ Collage Club
events.description:
THIS WEDNESDAY, MAY 20TH:

Join us for Collage Club in the Theater with joseph_rafferty 

Collage Club is a monthly community gathering at The Fruit. Rooted in Surrealism, we use provided materials - substrates (base), scissors, glue sticks, found objects, pre-cut images‚Äîto explore visual play, personification, and the subconscious. 

Each session begins with a theme and prompts designed to encourage "creating first and thinking later." All experience levels are welcome to join this creative laboratory where things that look like things become something else entirely!

Collage Club is a creative session lead by our friend and renowned creative mystic, Joseph Rafferty.

All needed materials are included with the session entry fee.

üìÖ Wed, May 20th
‚è∞ Ô∏è7-9p

events.title: üéµ MJ: The Essentials ‚Äî a live dance tribute
events.description:
THIS WEEKEND: rdhproductions presents
MJ: The Essentials ‚Äî a live dance tribute celebrating the legacy and artistry of Michael Jackson through a chronological showcase of music, dance, and visual storytelling.

Under the creative direction of Romell D. Harris, this is not just a tribute ‚Äî it‚Äôs a reimagined performance experience. Expect signature choreography, powerful storytelling, and a fresh spin on the MJ catalog. From the anthems you love to the most electrifying performance moments, this showcase will honor the legacy while pushing artistic boundaries.

This live performance experience features:
‚Ä¢ Timeline-based show (Jackson 5 era ‚Üí Thriller era ‚Üí 90s stadium era ‚Üí Legacy finale)
‚Ä¢ Theatrical transitions
‚Ä¢ Visual projections
‚Ä¢ Live monologues 
‚Ä¢ Originally reimagined choreography

It's here for a two-day matinee run with doors opening at 3:30pm both this afternoon and Sunday the 17th.

Show begins at 4pm.

Come learn & play at the Photo Jam! Hosted by Fruit resident artist Jess Cruger and renowned local photographer Michael Gillman, this skillshare  workshop invites you to partake in a playful hands-on environment with a focus on creative ways to use off-camera flash.

events.title: üéµ Go Figure Drawing Group
events.description:
NEXT MONDAY 5/4:

Always wanted to try out Figure Drawing? or perhaps you're looking to get back into the practice after some time off? Or.... you don't care too much for drawing but just want to meet local creatives in our natural habit. If any of this resonates with you, you're in luck. 

Go Figure is a drawing group founded to bring community together and make figure drawing accessible. This isn't a class but a chance to try something new out in a safe, supportive environment.

You are welcome to bring whatever drawing supplies you are currently finding inspiration in & ideally a mobile easel if you have one, but please, nothing that makes a mess... 
*ahem* NO dripping, squirting, or splashes. üé®üë®‚Äçüé® 

Doors open at 6:30. Drawing is from 7pm-9pm.
Mature audiences only. 18+ only.

*Absolutely NO photography of the model or the space while the event is active.*

events.title: üéµ Kink Practice Open House
events.description:
Hello HELLOOOO, what a delightful little last minute surprise we have in store for you HUMP heads here!

The lovely folks behind Kink Practice serve us a delicious Open House treat next Saturday.

Watch demonstrations by rope, fire, and other adult practitioners and learn how to get involved in the local community!

Meet and chat with locals who are excited to show off their skills (no audience participation permitted) and keep you entertained before the film festival begins. Check out the info booth to get linked up to classes and educational opportunities in the area.

poly_anthus
ties_by_oliver
hounds.tooth1

events.title: üåà Dancing With The Carolina Stars: Celebration
events.description:
Dancing with the Stars ‚Äì DURHAM edition! üåü

Michelle is officially competing in carolinatheatredurham‚Äôs Dancing With The Carolina Stars: Celebration on May 20! She‚Äôll be on stage with her dance partner Alyssa ascho3 supporting the theater‚Äôs mission during their biggest fundraising night of the year. 

While Michelle loves a dance party, Alyssa brings choreography to the table as one half of A+A Dance Company aandadanceco and co-director and co-producer for RECITAL recital_durham. Vote for this powerhouse team via donation to the Carolina Theatre! Link via carolinatheatredurham üíñ

Every ticket sold and donation/vote given supports the Carolina Theatre, one of Durham‚Äôs most beloved institutions that provides a platform that allows artists to entertain and express through the creation of emotional and educational experiences that reshape hearts and minds.

Come support the arts & all the folks dancing!

‚ÄúThis exhibition brings together the timeless wisdom of Aesop‚Äôs Fables and beloved children‚Äôs animal stories, reimagined through the intricate visual language of Gond-inspired art,‚Äù Sampada says. ‚ÄúRooted in the indigenous traditions of central India, Gond art is known for its rhythmic lines, detailed patterns, and deep connection to nature and storytelling.‚Äù

events.org: Chapel Hill Arts - Small Town Pride
events.description:
Save the date for the annual Chapel Hill-Carrboro Juneteenth Celebration!

On Friday, June 19 from 4‚Äì8pm, come to Hargraves Community Center to enjoy live music and line dancing, shop local vendors and food trucks, and celebrate Juneteenth with your neighbors.

Juneteenth, also known as Emancipation Day, commemorates the emancipation of enslaved people in the United States. The Towns of Chapel Hill and Carrboro proclaimed the observance and commemoration of Juneteenth on June 19, 2020 and encourage residents and employers to recognize the holiday.

events.title: üåà Exhibition by Voon Wong
events.org: Chapel Hill Arts - Small Town Pride
events.description:
Have you seen our latest exhibition by Voon Wong (artvw)? 

Exploring everyday interactions and drawing from his own personal experiences, Voon‚Äôs watercolors are rooted in memory, culture, and humanity. 
 
Voon discovered his passion for art in high school and studied at an art college in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, before earning a master‚Äôs degree from academy_of_art. His early watercolor paintings feature serene landscapes and local fishing boats, reflecting his connection to coastal life. Today, he focuses on capturing human experiences. In 2025, he was honored with Signature Member status in both the nwsgallery and the watercolorsocietync.
 
Come visit (it‚Äôs free!):
üî∏ On display until May 7
üî∏ Located at chapelhillpubliclibrary
üî∏ Mon-Thurs: 10am-8pm, Fri-Sun: 10am-6pm

events.description:
What are dreams and what do they mean? How do emotions shape our lives?
 
These are questions that artist brook.ramsey tries to answer in the latest exhibition at chapelhillgov Town Hall, called ‚ÄúBeyond the Surface.‚Äù
 
‚ÄúThis series is a story about how our lives are transformed by what we choose to take away from our experiences,‚Äù Brook says. ‚ÄúIt is also about the transformative power that honoring our emotions has in our lives.‚Äù

events.title: üéµ Bear Ghost
events.description:
NOW ON SALE! BEAR GHOST - Sat Oct 3 @ MOTORCO

with SARAH & THE SAFE WORD / SPACEMAN BOB

GET TIX @ motorcomusic.com/event/bear-ghost

andmoreagainpresents 

"BIOS ARE FOR THE WEAK!" Bear Ghost cried as they meekly tapped out a Spotify bio, pausing every minute or so to recover from a productive sob. Productive in the sense of how many fluid ounces of mucin, glucose, sodium, etc. they produced, but little else. Their fingers pruned to the bone from the tears veiling the keyboard they feverishly clicked away at.

The room was dim. An overhead light flickered and danced to an eclectic rhythm of a song that had not yet been written. But would be written. By Bear Ghost, probably.

"Why must you torture yourselves?" mewled their manager, fearing for their sanity as they neared day 11 without sleep. His fingers pruned to the bone from his fondest hobby: sundrying plums with his bare hands.

Bear Ghost rose to their feet, eyes swollen. They looked calm now. Resigned but resolute. "How else will they know?" they pleaded, faltering as their knees begged to give way. "How else will they know that Bear Ghost is an Adventure Rock band that fuses fun and quirky musicianship with complex melodies, phat harmonies, heavy riffitude, and pop structure to deliver a sound purposely crafted to fill all booties, tall and small, with righteous boogie?"

The room was still, but undeniably changed. The air somehow tingling. Light and effervescent with the anticipation of something new, something fresh, something that could maybe, just maybe, save the whole wide world.

"You did it," choked their stunned manager. "I'll get the president on the phone."
